survivors_flagged.one_line: Live Kimberly-Clark merger, HSR expired Feb-4 and shareholders approved Jan-29, expected H2-2026 close — clean dated merger-arb spread.
Pending all-cash take-private at $10.25 by McMorrow/Fairfax consortium (no Q1 call due to deal) — dated take-private arb.

All-stock merger with Treeline (H2-2026 close, shareholder/regulatory pending) + active divestiture of legacy mass-cytometry/microfluidics, ~$900M pro-forma cash; structural but stock-deal economics already partly set.
Live arb: Charter buys Liberty Broadband at 0.236 CHTR/sh, closing mid-2026 contemporaneous w/ Cox; FCC-approved, only CA left, HSR hard deadline Sep 15 2026; GCI spin condition already cleared.
Live arb: Somnigroup acquiring Leggett & Platt all-stock (Apr-26, 0.1455 SYG/sh, LEG holders ~9% of NewCo); definitive deal pending close.

Two dated 2H2026 structural events: $845M Aerojet Space Propulsion majority sale to AEI + Missile Solutions IPO/spinoff with Pentagon as $1B anchor; real but value-relative-modest at a $50B+ mega-cap.

Formal strategic review with full sale on the table; activists Ananym + Ancora (board seats); active divestiture program (self-service $410M done, specialty ~$1B process, Europe sale pushed) — strong multi-prong special situation.
Definitive all-stock merger into Burke & Herbert at ~$9.38/sh (trading $8.69), expected Q2 2026 close — clean dated bank merger-arb, regulatory/vote approvals pending.

Live unsolicited Biglari take-private offer (15.1% holder, confidentiality agreement signed, board reviewing w/ advisors, rights plan adopted) + CapitalSpring 5.2% activist; forward merger-arb/going-private with mispriced asymmetry, not yet fired.
Lexicon Zynquista (sotagliflozin) NDA resubmission on track for mid-2026 with potential T1D approval by year-end — dated binary with asymmetry on a sub-$3 stock, CRL'd once.

Lifezone Kabanga nickel: dated late-2026 FID plus near-term project-finance/offtake closings (SocGen-led, DFC/multilateral, BHP stake consolidated) — binary milestone with asymmetry on a dev-stage miner.
Veradermics (Feb-2026 IPO): Phase 3 '304' oral-minoxidil topline due H2 2026; dated binary, but '302' already positive so de-risked and re-rated — asymmetry partly spent.
Definitive Danaher deal at $180 cash ($9.9B); shareholders approved May-1-2026, regulatory close H2 2026 — clean merger-arb spread still open.
Barington 2nd proxy contest + SGK divestiture ($350M) and warehouse-automation sale ($230M) funding 2.5x delever, 2026 annual meeting/governance vote — dated structural catalyst.
Maze Tx: additional HORIZON (MZE829 AMKD) data late-2026/early-2027 + two MZE782 Ph2 starts mid/H2-2026; dated developmental readouts, not a mispriced special-sit binary.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Kimberly-Clark acquisition of Kenvue ($3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB shares/share) closing into a live ~2.2% gross arb spread pending FTC second-request and EU merger clearance.
verified.dated_milestone: FTC second-request review / EU merger approval to complete; deal close targeted H2-2026 (next gate: FTC clearance, 2026)
verified.instrument: KVUE common (long) vs KMB short hedge of 0.14625 ratio; or KVUE outright on deal completion
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty KMB, definitive agreement), G2 (concrete commitment: both shareholder votes passed Jan-29-2026, HSR initial period expired Feb-4-2026, EU FSR cleared Jun-9-2026), G3 (currently-unpriced ~2.2% gross / ~5-9% annualized spread reflecting open regulatory gates)
verified.edge: M — real dated spread but consumer-staples deal under active FTC second-request review caps the unpriced return at low-double-digit annualized; reward modest, residual antitrust/EU-clearance risk is the offsetting downside.
verified.primary_source: Kimberly-Clark/Kenvue joint shareholder-approval release (Jan-29-2026) + DEFM14A merger proxy ($3.50 + 0.14625 KMB/share); MLex EU FSR clearance (Jun-9-2026); FMP quotes KVUE $18.935 / KMB $108.46 (2026-06-29)
verified.kill_fact: Definitive deal terms confirmed via primary source (KMB Form 425 / StockTitan): $3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB shares per KVUE share. At live KMB $108.49, implied value = $3.50 + 0.14625*108.49 = $19.37 vs KVUE $18.925 → gross spread ~2.3% (triage's ~2.2% is accurate). Shareholders approved Jan-29-2026 (~96% KMB / ~99% KVUE); EU Foreign-Subsidies cleared ~Jun-8/9-2026. CORRECTION: the deal is in FTC SECOND-REQUEST review and standard EU MERGER approval is STILL PENDING — so HSR did NOT cleanly clear (a second request extends the waiting period; the 'HSR expired = clean' framing is wrong, which the triage itself flags). Close targeted H2-2026. This is a genuine forward (not fired/priced/story) merger-arb, ACTIVE and tradeable (liquid US large-cap pair, KVUE long vs 0.14625 KMB short). Edge is real but modest: ~2.2% gross / ~5-9% annualized against live second-request antitrust risk. No fatal disqualifier.
verified.analysis: Verified live: $48.7B KMB/KVUE deal has definitive agreement, ~96%/~99% shareholder approvals (Jan-29-2026), HSR initial waiting period expired Feb-4-2026, and EU Foreign-Subsidies clearance (Jun-9-2026); close targeted H2-2026. At KMB $108.46 the $3.50 + 0.14625-share consideration implies $19.36 vs KVUE $18.935 — a ~2.2% gross spread (~5-9% annualized) that persists because the FTC issued a second request and EU merger approval is still pending, so the deal is NOT yet cleared (the triage's "HSR expired = clean" overstates certainty). Clean dated forward merger-arb, ACTIVE, but edge is medium given modest return vs residual regulatory risk.

verified.catalyst: Kennedy-Wilson take-private by the McMorrow/Fairfax consortium has already CLOSED at $10.90/share (the $10.25 in the flag was the stale initial Nov offer).
verified.dated_milestone: Deal closed 2026-06-16; stockholder approval 2026-06-10; Form 25-NSE delisting filed — no forward date remains.
verified.bloom_gates: none — fails FORWARD gate (catalyst fully fired/realized)
verified.edge: L — arb spread is closed; consideration paid in cash, shares delisting, no residual upside to capture
verified.primary_source: Fairfax Financial press release "Fairfax Announces Completion of Kennedy Wilson Take-Private Transaction" + KW Form 25-NSE, dated 2026-06-16; Globe and Mail (initial $10.25 vs final $10.90), 2026
verified.analysis: The flagged catalyst is stale and fired: the consortium's initial $10.25/share November proposal was sweetened to a final $10.90/share, stockholders approved on 2026-06-10, and Fairfax announced completion of the take-private on 2026-06-16, with a Form 25-NSE delisting notice filed. There is no remaining forward, unpriced milestone — the merger-arb spread has fully collapsed to the closed deal price and KW is exiting the public market. This fails the FORWARD gate decisively (G1 counterparty named but commitment already consummated), so it is not actionable.

verified.catalyst: All-stock merger into private oncology co. Treeline (LAB holders get ~16% of combined co + 1 CVR/share for legacy mass-cyto/microfluidics divestiture proceeds and up to $50M Illumina/SomaLogic earnout), expected to close H2-2026 pending LAB shareholder and regulatory approval.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote + regulatory approval; deal expected to close H2-2026 (announced 2026-06-08; next: special meeting/proxy in coming months, Nasdaq min-bid deadline 2026-10-19).
verified.instrument: equity (LAB common; converts to TRLN + CVR at close)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Treeline) + G3 (concrete unpriced CVR: legacy divestiture proceeds + up to $50M Illumina earnout)
verified.edge: L — LAB trades $0.73 / ~$286M mkt cap vs ~$450M net cash it contributes, but the discount is rational: post-close holders are minority (~16%) owners of private clinical-stage Treeline equity whose realizable value is unknowable, not a quantifiable mispricing; CVR upside (incl. $50M Illumina earnout) is real but small and undated. Stock already re-rated DOWN hard (year-high $1.72 → $0.73).
verified.primary_source: Standard BioTools/Treeline merger press release, GlobeNewswire 2026-06-08; FMP quote LAB $0.73 (2026-06-29); Nasdaq min-bid deficiency note (deadline 2026-10-19, StockTitan).
verified.analysis: LAB is being absorbed by private oncology developer Treeline in an all-stock deal where LAB holders end up with ~16% of the combined $900M-cash company plus one CVR per share tied to legacy mass-cytometry/microfluidics divestiture proceeds and up to $50M of Illumina/SomaLogic earnout. The headline "trades below its own ~$450M net cash" looks like a gap, but it isn't a clean spread: the swap converts shareholders into minority owners of an illiquid private biotech, so the market's discount reflects genuine uncertainty about Treeline's equity value rather than a fixable mispricing. It clears WATCH as a real, dated, forward, named-counterparty event (vote + reg approval, H2-2026 close, not yet fired), but the edge is Low because the only truly quantifiable unpriced piece (the CVR/earnout) is small versus market cap and the core upside is a speculative private-equity valuation, not an arbitrageable figure.

verified.catalyst: Charter acquires Liberty Broadband at a fixed 0.236 CHTR/share ratio, with the close accelerated to be contemporaneous with the Charter-Cox merger rather than the June 30, 2027 outside date.
verified.dated_milestone: California CPUC decision targeted by ~July 16, 2026 (last approval needed); DOJ HSR clearance hard-expires Sep 15, 2026; LBRDA close then follows contemporaneously with Charter-Cox.
verified.instrument: equity (LBRDA common; arb = long LBRDA / short 0.236 CHTR)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Charter, definitive merger agreement) + G2 (binding 0.236 fixed exchange ratio with acceleration-to-Cox commitment); G3 fails — residual spread is already compressed
verified.edge: L — gross spread is only ~0.8% (deal value 0.236 x $148.60 = ~$35.07 vs LBRDA $34.80), and both LBRDA and CHTR gapped +11% today, i.e. the catalyst is actively re-rating/being priced, not sitting unpriced; thin spread to a ~mid-2026 close offers little annualized edge for the residual deal/timing risk.
verified.primary_source: Liberty Broadband press release "Liberty Broadband To Accelerate Closing Of Its Acquisition By Charter" + Charter/Cox SEC DEFM14A; FCC Charter-Cox approval Feb 27, 2026; CA CPUC/HSR timing per broadbandbreakfast.com (June 2026). LBRDA/CHTR quotes via FMP 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: The merger-arb is real and dated: 0.236 fixed ratio confirmed, GCI spin condition already cleared (July 14, 2025), Charter-Cox FCC-approved (Feb 27, 2026), with only California CPUC left (targeted ~July 16, 2026) ahead of the Sep 15, 2026 HSR hard stop, and LBRDA's close accelerated to be contemporaneous with Cox. But the structural catalyst is largely fired/priced: today's +11% co-move in both names and a compressed ~0.8% gross spread mean the unpriced upside (Bloom G3) is gone. The triage flag's premise is otherwise accurate, but the residual is a low-edge, near-completed arb, not an 8-conviction ACTIVE setup.

verified.catalyst: Somnigroup (SGI) acquiring Leggett & Platt in a definitive all-stock deal at a fixed 0.14555 SGI/share exchange ratio (LEG holders ~9% of NewCo), targeted to close by year-end 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: LEG special shareholder meeting in 2026 (date still blank in S-4/proxy); closing targeted by year-end 2026; next hard date = vote date to be set in definitive proxy.
verified.instrument: Equity (LEG common; arb = long LEG / short 0.14555 SGI)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty SGI) + G2 (concrete definitive merger agreement, fixed ratio, board-approved, HSR cleared)
verified.edge: L — spread is negative: LEG $11.515 vs deal-implied 0.14555 x SGI $77.80 = $11.325, i.e. ~-1.7%; the arb edge is gone/inverted, so riding the long-LEG/short-SGI spread to close loses money. Likely reflects the 6% formulaic special-dividend optionality, not a capturable mispricing.
verified.primary_source: SEC Form 425 / S-4 (Somnigroup-Leggett & Platt) + StockTitan deal announcement (Apr 13, 2026); HSR clearance per Woodworking Network (Jun 3, 2026); live FMP quotes LEG $11.515 / SGI $77.80 (Jun 29, 2026).
verified.analysis: The deal is real, dated, and forward (definitive agreement Apr 13 2026, both boards unanimous, US HSR period expired Jun 3 2026, close targeted year-end 2026), so G1 and G2 are cleanly met. But for merger-arb purposes the catalyst is effectively fired/re-rated: LEG ($11.515) trades ~1.7% ABOVE the exchange-ratio-implied value (0.14555 x SGI $77.80 = $11.325), so the spread is negative and there is no positive long-LEG/short-SGI edge to harvest into close. The only un-priced optionality is the contingent 6% special dividend and a (low-probability) topping bid, which the market may already be partly capitalizing into the inverted spread.

verified.catalyst: Two dated 2H2026 structural unlocks at L3Harris: the $845M majority sale of Space Propulsion (Rocketdyne) to AE Industrial Partners awaiting close, and the IPO carve-out of Missile Solutions (ex-Aerojet solid rocket motors) with the Pentagon as a $1B anchor.

verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: AE Industrial Partners on propulsion; DoW/Pentagon as $1B anchor + JPMorgan/Morgan Stanley underwriting the IPO), G2 (concrete commitments: signed AEI SPA, S-1 draft registration filed), G3 ($845M EV / 60-40 split; ~80% retained MSL stake; $1B convertible-preferred)
verified.edge: M — both events are real, specific, dated and forward (propulsion sale unclosed; IPO not yet priced), but value-relative-modest against a ~$50B+ mega-cap: a small minority monetization plus a sub-IPO LHX keeps ~80% of. The lever is sum-of-parts visibility/de-conglomeration, not a transformational unlock; the $1B Pentagon piece already closed (partly fired).
verified.primary_source: L3Harris press releases: "Confidential Submission of Draft Registration Statement" (Apr 29 2026) and "Closes $1B Investment from Dept of War in Missile Solutions" (Apr 2026); SpaceNews/L3Harris on $845M AEI propulsion sale (Jan 2026)
verified.analysis: Verified: L3Harris agreed Jan 2026 to sell a 60% majority of Space Propulsion/Power (Rocketdyne) to AE Industrial Partners at $845M EV, retaining 40%, expected to close 2H2026 (unclosed). Separately, the Missile Solutions (ex-Aerojet SRM) carve-out filed a confidential draft IPO registration Apr 29 2026 with JPMorgan/Morgan Stanley for a 2H2026 launch, with the Pentagon's $1B convertible-preferred anchor investment already CLOSED in April 2026 — so the IPO and propulsion close are the still-forward catalysts, while the $1B funding is fired. Genuine dated SoP/de-conglomeration catalysts, but on a $50B+ mega-cap the re-rating magnitude is modest, supporting WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Board running a formal strategic review including a full sale of the company (BofA/Wachtell engaged) plus an active ~$1B specialty/Keystone divestiture, under Ananym + Ancora activist pressure with Ancora board seats.
verified.dated_milestone: No company-set timetable ("no deadline or definitive timetable" per the Jan 26 release); next likely update is Q2 2026 earnings, ~late July/early Aug 2026. Annual meeting (May 6, 2026) and self-service $410M sale already fired.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 only (concrete commitment: board-authorized formal sale process with named top-tier advisors). G1 fails — no named acquirer/counterparty. G3 partial — ~$1B specialty figure is sourced, but no dated, company-confirmed sale price.
verified.primary_source: LKQ 8-K / IR press release "LKQ Initiates Strategic Review to Maximize Shareholder Value," Jan 26, 2026 (sec.gov / investor.lkqcorp.com); price via FMP quote, $26.38, 2026-06-29. Activist/process detail corroborated by AInvest "LKQ Activist Push for Sale" and StockTitan.
verified.analysis: LKQ is a genuine multi-prong special situation: a Jan 26, 2026 board review explicitly including a full company sale (BofA + Wachtell), an ongoing ~$1B Keystone/specialty sale process, a banked $410M self-service divestiture, and activists Ananym + Ancora (Ancora holds board seats) pushing portfolio simplification. The thesis is real and the situation un-fired, with the stock near 52-week lows (no re-rate), implying the sale/breakup optionality is not priced in. However it fails the strict dated-forward-catalyst test: the company states there is NO deadline or timetable, there is no named counterparty, and the only firm dated events (May 6 annual meeting, self-service sale) have already fired — so it is a WATCH/spread-recovery candidate awaiting a concrete sale announcement or Q2-earnings (~late July 2026) update, not an ACTIVE dated event.

verified.catalyst: Lexicon plans to resubmit the Zynquista (sotagliflozin) NDA for type 1 diabetes in mid-2026 with potential FDA approval before year-end, a dated binary on a sub-$3 stock that was CRL'd once (Dec 2024).
verified.dated_milestone: NDA resubmission targeted mid-2026 (next ~Q3 2026); potential approval before YE-2026. No PDUFA date set (resubmission not yet filed). Gating data event = STENO1 exposure/safety readout.
verified.instrument: equity (common, NASDAQ:LXRX) — sub-$3 binary; options thin/illiquid on a $1.1B-cap micro-name
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: FDA + Steno Diabetes Center, the third-party STENO1 sponsor whose dataset gates resubmission); G2 (concrete dated commitment: mid-2026 NDA resubmission for T1D conditioned on a defined FDA exposure/safety threshold)
verified.edge: M — Real dated forward binary with genuine asymmetry on a sub-$3 name CRL'd once, but the timeline hinges entirely on STENO1, an investigator-initiated trial Lexicon does NOT control hitting FDA-set exposure/safety thresholds, and no firm resubmission/PDUFA date exists yet. Stock has already re-rated from $0.80 yearLow to $2.57 (200d avg $1.55), so some approval optionality is priced.
verified.primary_source: Lexicon Pharmaceuticals Q1 2026 results (May 7, 2026) + JPM business/pipeline update (Jan 12, 2026); corroborated by PharmaNow report on mid-2026 resubmission (May 2026). FMP quote 2026-06-29: LXRX $2.565.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed against primary sources. Lexicon JPM update (Jan 12, 2026) and Q1 2026 release (May 7, 2026): on track for Zynquista (sotagliflozin) T1D NDA resubmission and potential regulatory approval in 2026, following the Dec 2024 CRL (DKA-risk concern) — a genuine dated forward binary, not yet fired, not a macro bet. FMP quote confirms instrument: $2.5664 sub-$3 (yearLow $0.80, marketCap ~$1.14B, 200d avg $1.55), partial re-rating already underway so catalyst is live. Conviction correctly capped: the entire regulatory clock is gated on STENO1 — a third-party-funded, investigator-initiated study run by Steno Diabetes Center (Denmark) that Lexicon does NOT control — meeting FDA patient-exposure/safety thresholds; no resubmission or PDUFA date is locked, and the timing language is explicitly conditional ('if requirements are achieved'). The 'mid-2026' resubmission is a soft target dependent on an uncontrolled external trial, not an agency-set date — this external-dependency overhang is the reason a 6 (not higher) is correct. ACTIVE/score 6 holds.
verified.analysis: VERIFIED. Lexicon's Q1 2026 release (May 7, 2026) and the Jan 2026 JPM update confirm the Zynquista (sotagliflozin) T1D NDA resubmission is on track for mid-2026 with potential approval before YE-2026, following the Dec 2024 CRL — a genuine, dated, forward binary, not yet fired and not a macro bet. The asymmetry is real on a $2.57 sub-$3 stock (yearLow $0.80, marketcap ~$1.14B), but conviction is capped because the entire regulatory clock depends on STENO1 — a third-party-funded, investigator-initiated study run by Denmark's Steno Diabetes Center that Lexicon doesn't control — meeting FDA patient-exposure and safety thresholds, and no resubmission/PDUFA date is locked yet. The run from $0.80 to $2.57 (200d avg $1.55) shows partial re-rating, so the catalyst is live and dated but with a material external-dependency overhang; ACTIVE/score 6 holds.

verified.kill_fact: Primary source (Lifezone IR release, Dec 11 2025) confirms the spine verbatim: "Mid-2026: Targeted FID for Kabanga Nickel Project and financial close of multi-source funding package"; DFC "Environmental and Social public consultation and due diligence completed"; offtake "no agreements have been reached"; $75M H2-2025 ($60M Taurus bridge closed Sep 2 2025 + $15M offering Nov 12 2025) "fully funding current pre-FID activities." Société Générale (debt) / Standard Chartered (equity, term-sheet stage, multiple offers, uncommitted) / DFC anchor confirmed via Q1 2026 results + Crux (Mar 30 2026). Genuine dated forward company-specific binary, not fired, G1 clears. CORRECTIONS: (1) decisive equity solution explicitly uncommitted — Crux quotes CFO "we probably have to find or enter some form of a partnership again for the equity check," equity is a condition that "must be satisfied" before FID; (2) secondary coverage (Crux Mar 30 2026) repeatedly frames it as the "late-2026 FID," confirming the line's own slippage caveat; (3) NOT in the line and worsening the overhang — a further $25M registered direct offering closed Apr 23 2026 (5.7M shares @ $4.40), on top of the $75M; cash only $15.3M at Mar 31 2026 (down from $20.1M), so dilution/financing overhang is heavier than stated and pre-FID liquidity is thin. Stock $4.85 (Jun 18 2026) vs $3.07 52-wk low — near-low/overhang framing holds; close is unpriced, not a pure nickel-macro bet. Held to 6: equity uncommitted, offtake unsigned, late-2026 slippage risk, dev-stage African-miner execution risk.
verified.analysis: LZM is a genuine dated, forward, company-specific binary: a Societe Generale-led debt package, Standard Chartered-run equity/strategic-investor process, and DFC anchor (E&S diligence completed Q1 2026) all converging on a targeted mid-2026 FID and financial close, with $75M of H2-2025 raises ($60M Taurus bridge + $15M offering) funding pre-FID work. It clears G1 (named counterparties) and is not yet fired — the stock sits near its 52-week low under heavy dilution/financing overhang, so the close is unpriced rather than re-rated, and it is not a pure nickel-macro bet. Held to ACTIVE/6 rather than higher because the decisive equity solution is uncommitted, long-term offtake is explicitly "no agreements reached," and secondary coverage already hints at slippage toward late-2026 — execution and timing risk on a dev-stage African miner are material.

verified.dated_milestone: '304' confirmatory Phase 3 topline — H2 2026 (no firm date; enrollment completed Feb 9 2026, 24-wk co-primary, likely Q3-Q4 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-priced-but-binary figure: dated confirmatory Phase 3 readout, 536 men, co-primary hair-count + patient-reported coverage at 24wk)
verified.edge: L — asymmetry largely spent: positive '302' (Apr 27 2026) already de-risked the same drug/indication/endpoints and drove the re-rate (IPO $17 Feb 2026 → ~$127, +175% YoY, ~$5.3B cap); '304' is confirmatory so residual unpriced upside is thin (mean PT $132 vs $127 spot ≈ 4%) and the live risk is a surprise-miss downside tail.
verified.primary_source: Veradermics IR / company clinical-trials page + BusinessWire '302' release (Apr 27 2026) and enrollment-complete '304' release (Feb 2026); stock/analyst data via StockAnalysis/Yahoo (Jun 29 2026)
verified.analysis: MANE is Veradermics (the triage "Veradermics" and "MANE" are the same name); it IPO'd Feb 4 2026 at $17 and trades ~$126.91 (~$5.3B cap) after a positive Phase 2/3 '302' readout on Apr 27 2026 that re-rated the stock. The remaining dated forward binary is the confirmatory Phase 3 '304' (536 men, co-primary endpoints at 24 weeks, enrollment completed Feb 9 2026) with topline guided to H2 2026 — a genuine un-fired event, but because it merely confirms an already-positive predecessor trial on the same drug/endpoints, the unpriced upside is mostly spent and the live exposure is downside-skewed; analysts' mean PT ($132) sits only ~4% above spot. Gap: company guidance is only "H2 2026" with no firm date (one aggregator's "July 1 2026" is unconfirmed and contradicts the IR/SEC language), so the milestone is soft-dated; female Phase 2/3 (NCT07146022) is recruiting with no timeline — keeps it WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Danaher's $180/share all-cash acquisition of Masimo has already closed and MASI is delisted — no open arb spread remains.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal CLOSED June 10, 2026; MASI delisted from Nasdaq. No forward date remains.
verified.edge: L — catalyst fully realized and re-rated; merger completed, stock no longer trades, spread closed.
verified.primary_source: panabee.com / StockTitan 8-K "Danaher Completes $180 Per Share Acquisition of Masimo" — close date June 10, 2026 (accessed 2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: The triage flag is stale: it describes the Danaher/Masimo merger as an open arb spread with H2 2026 regulatory close, but Danaher actually COMPLETED the $180/share all-cash acquisition on June 10, 2026, ~19 days ago, and MASI was delisted from Nasdaq. The merger agreement (Feb 16, 2026) was approved by shareholders May 1, 2026 and has fully closed, so there is no remaining forward, unfired, datable catalyst and no tradable instrument. This is a fired event, not an active or watch candidate.

verified.catalyst: Barington's 2nd proxy contest and the 2026 annual-meeting governance vote that the thesis hinges on have already resolved — Barington withdrew its nominations in a Jan 15, 2026 settlement and the meeting was held Feb 19, 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: 2026 Annual Meeting held Feb 19, 2026 (PAST); Barington settlement Jan 15, 2026 (PAST); Warehouse Automation/Duravant $230M sale expected to close by end of fiscal Q2 2026 (~end Mar 2026, PAST). No forward-dated milestone remains.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: Barington, Propelis, Duravant) + G2 (concrete commitments: $350M SGK sale, $230M warehouse sale) are technically met, but ALL are already consummated/announced and re-rated — no G3 forward unpriced figure with a future date remains.
verified.edge: L — the activist/governance and divestiture events are in the past and priced (stock $27.13, near 50/200d avgs, off the $30.93 52w high but well above the $21.95 low); the Barington overhang is resolved, removing the catalyst rather than creating a forward one.
verified.primary_source: Matthews International press release "Provides Updates Following Its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders," Feb 19, 2026 (matw.com /detail/305); and "Provides Update on Actions to Create Shareholder Value," Dec 7, 2025 (matw.com /detail/297) confirming Duravant close target end of fiscal Q2 2026 and Jan 15, 2026 Barington settlement.
verified.analysis: The triage flag describes a real, well-documented activist/breakup situation (Barington 2nd proxy contest, SGK $350M sale to Propelis, Warehouse Automation $230M sale to Duravant, 2.5x delever target), but every dated leg has already fired as of today (June 29, 2026): Barington settled and withdrew nominations on Jan 15, 2026, the 2026 annual meeting and governance vote occurred Feb 19, 2026 (new chair Nauman seated, board declassification adopted), and the Duravant close was slated for end of fiscal Q2 2026. With the proxy overhang removed and divestitures consummated, there is no forward, un-fired, currently-unpriced dated catalyst — only ongoing strategic-review execution, which is open-ended and not a dated event. Gating strictly: catalyst fired, no forward dated milestone, so tier NONE / score 2.
